scope: General scaffold for DRL, ADP, and RL solutions to MDP, POMDP, CMDP, and multi-agent MDP problems.
runnability: Not runnable by default. The future task must implement environment, data, networks, algorithm, runner, and scripts as needed.

directories.algorithms: Catalogs and contracts for single-agent, multi-agent, offline, imitation, model-based, and planning RL algorithms.
directories.data: Contracts for data loaders, offline trajectories, scenario generators, replay imports, and exogenous process inputs.
directories.envs: Contracts for MDP, POMDP, CMDP, and multi-agent environment definitions.
directories.networks: Contracts for policy, value, Q, critic, centralized critic, encoder, recurrent, attention, and world-model modules.
directories.runners: Contracts for rollout collection, training loops, evaluation loops, checkpointing, and foreground run execution.
directories.scripts: Foreground script templates for train, eval, user-facing run scripts, and compact result collection.
directories.run_records: Templates for run config, status, progress, artifacts, and convergence records.

industrial_quality_policy: Do not use this asset to generate toy or minimal RL examples.
A deployment must implement task-specific state, action, reward, transition, feasibility, baseline, evaluation, progress-output, and run-record contracts.
Training must periodically evaluate on held-out seeds or scenarios, save the best checkpoint, and use that checkpoint for final evaluation by default.

A pilot must show meaningful learning or objective improvement against a strong baseline before recommending a user-facing `run`; a weak first pilot should trigger targeted redesign attempts when budget allows.

Training deployments must define a credible fallback policy or method before pilot runs and evaluate it under the same protocol if learning is unstable, underperforms, or exhausts the redesign budget.
